attacking_style: Pure sprinter who does not attack in traditional sense. Wins from final 200m in bunch sprints, often by positioning on rival wheels rather than leading out early.
team_tactics: FDJ commits full leadout train even when domestiques are fatigued. However, Démare shows adaptability by abandoning team positioning to freelance on rival wheels when beneficial, as seen multiple times in Giro sprints.
terrain_preference: Flat bunch sprints where selections are not overly aggressive. Survives moderate climbs when tempo is controlled but vulnerable to hard accelerations on penultimate climbs that could isolate him from team support.
vulnerability: Pre-stage tactical pressure via high tempo on penultimate climbs can temporarily isolate him. Relies on selections not being decisive enough to prevent regrouping after mechanicals or positioning errors.
key_patterns: Opportunistically targets intermediate sprints when rivals conserve energy, gaining bonus seconds uncontested.
Capable of chasing back after mechanicals/punctures when race tempo insufficient to create definitive selections.
Switches from team leadout to wheel-surfing tactics mid-finale when chaos or positioning favors freelancing.
French team overcommits domestique resources even on non-priority stages, potentially depleting support for key moments.
form_trajectory: positive: 2022 Giro shows tactical evolution, winning by intelligent wheel selection rather than pure leadout dependence
